titulo: Georeferenced Multipurpose Real Estate Re-registration Support System
abstract: The implantation and maintenance of a cartographic base is not a simple task, mainly in great territorial extensions, such as for example, Brazil. Cities follow a dynamic system of growth, undergoing significant changes in a relatively short period, which makes urban mapping a major challenge. Outdated cadastral bases make it difficult for managers to take decisions and tax taxes that make up a large part of the municipalities budget. The objective of this paper is to present a support system for the georeferenced multipurpose real estate re-registration of cities. The system takes into account images collected by drones during overflights in cities. These images are vectorized and the data obtained are compared with the cadastral data provided by the city hall, generating an updated geographic database. In addition, the system is composed of a web application with two modules, Administrative and Citizen, and an application for field collection, Collect Module. Through these modules, managers can analyze the updated geographic database to take strategic decisions about the city and tax taxation fairly. These definitions are in line with Brazilian legislation, following the premises defined in Ordinance number 511 of the Ministry of Cities and in Complementary Law number 101. RESUMO: .

titulo: Characterization of Fracture Geometry with Computed Microtomography Rock Images
abstract: Fractures are planar structures that can be found inside a rock, in which there is the ability to accumulate and transport fluids. From this important structure, we developed a computational framework that analyzes computed tomographic images by extracting their geometric characteristics. For this analysis, image processing techniques were developed that seek to identify the structure of the contours obtained through data segmentation. Thus, this work presents the techniques and algorithms that were implemented for the evaluation of these parameters, with the main objective of helping the reservoir area and fluid characterization, avoiding destructive analysis of the samples and reducing the uncertainties inherent to the characterization of the productive life of a field.

titulo: Territorial relations between deforestation and Rural Environmental Registry (CAR) in the Amazon biome using free software QGIS/PostgreSQL/PostGIS and Data Warehouse Structure
abstract: This work presents the use and integration of free software to organize a spatial database (part of a data warehouse) capable of performing spatial and numerical analyses on a set of spatial data from the Amazon biome (deforestation and Rural Environmental Registry - CAR). This work is part of a larger research project (Embrapa/Censipam) that addresses the use of strategic territorial intelligence to analyze deforestation processes in the Amazon biome. More specifically, this article deals with a part of the Data Warehouse construction that serves as the basis for the larger research project to work. The focus is on the spatial part of the data warehouse, proposing a free software structure to perform spatial analysis. As preliminary results, quantifications of deforestation by state and by municipalities are presented.

titulo: Exploring the diversity of informal settlements in Brazil: a clustering approach to qualitative field survey data
abstract: Upgrading informal settlements and improving the living conditions of their dwellers is still a challenge. In addition, it is important to produce upto- date information about them. This exploratory research aimed at expanding the knowledge on informal settlements through the identification, description and comparison of typologies present in Brazil. It relied on an extensive qualitative field survey applied to 157 municipalities in six regions. The territorial scope and format of data introduced methodological challenges that were addressed by using an ensemble k-modes clustering approach. The results illustrate the diversity of informal settlements through four typologies with distinctive characteristics.

titulo: FMASK algorithm applied to Landsat images: a study case in the region of Santarï¿½m, Parï¿½, Brazil
abstract: Clouds and cloud shadows are recurrent problems in optical remote sensing images. Algorithms have been developed for automatic detection of these targets such as the Function of Mask (Fmask) model. This work presents an automatic mapping of cloud areas with Fmask in Landsat images between 2017 and 2018 in northern Santarï¿½m, Parï¿½, Brazil. The results show that the algorithm presents a good performance (85%) in the study area and is a powerful tool in clouds and cloud shadows detection. However, for further works, it is suggested to use data at a larger time scale and the evaluation of more pixels within the images, when comparing Fmask with other algorithms.

titulo: A Survey on Persistence Strategies for Semantically Enriched Trajectories
abstract: Mobile object databases have been studied for at least 20 years. However, with the emergence of Big Data, new challenges for MODs were introduced, such as the ability to manage a large volume of data, support for low-latency spatiotemporal queries, the need for high insert throughput, and association from complex and heterogeneous data to raw trajectories. This last challenge gives rise to the concept of semantically enriched trajectories, i.e., trajectories with one or more associated semantic contexts, such as the speed of the movement, the points of interest on which the trajectory passes and the weather condition during the movement. This work presents a survey of recent proposals for the persistence of semantically enriched trajectories in databases using different technologies such as NoSQL, relational and triplestore. The characteristics raised and analyzed are the implementation project, taking into account the storage strategy of these trajectories, the database platform used and the considered data models. For the best of our knowledge, there is no survey in the literature with focus on persistence of semantic trajectories.

titulo: A QGIS plugin for BONDS project: integrating field data with geographical, remote sensing and health information
abstract: Open source Geographic Information System (GIS) have been fostering spatial data research such as Earth observation and environmental monitoring for more than 30 years. More recently, globally available geospatial information combined with web technologies are providing new environments and tools for data handling. Thus, binding the mapping and processing capabilities of traditional GIS to the accessibility and reliability of web-based data providers can bring new opportunities for research. In this paper, we built a QGIS plugin to explore the integration of different public data providers in Brazil along with field data produced by the BONDS project. The biOdiversity conservatioN with Development in Amazon wetlandS project (BONDS) proposes to develop biodiversity scenarios for the Amazonian floodplains aiming to support solutions to preserve biodiversity and ecosystem services. The use of web services enabled dynamic and fast access to several products ranging from remote sensing images, land use and land cover, territorial cartography, water quality, to COVID-19 health data, and more.

titulo: Remote Sensing and Machine Learning on Anomaly Detection at high spectral and temporal dynamics regions in Brazil
abstract: In climate changes context Remote Sensing tools are widely used and widespread in research. In this sense, Artificial Intelligence rises offering possible improves for environmental monitoring applications using techniques such as Machine Learning for Anomaly Detection applied to Remote Sensing imagery to identify the spatio-temporal changes over the Earths surface. This approach is explored in three high dynamic regions in Brazil assessing deforestation, fires and technological disaster areas using One-Class SVM and Isolation Forest methods over MODIS, Landsat and Sentinel images.

titulo: Application of GEOBIA for urban area classification using optical images from the PE3D project
abstract: The urban space is complex and has great variability of targets. The methods of extracting information, automated or manual, present limitations and difficulties. Although high resolution images allow better visibility of urban targets, there is a difficulty in automatically discriminating targets. The use of auxiliary layers and pre-processing in the image can help in the classification process. This work presents a semi-automation of feature extraction to assist in the vectorization of blocks, streets and lots. A subdivision in Caruaru, Pernambuco, was used as a test area. The results can help in the Processed Product block and saved on the basis of COMPESA (Companhia Pernambucana de Saneamento).

titulo: Database of 2019 Brazilian oil disaster: an overview of a dataset and its application on an Artificial Intelligence monitoring system
abstract: In September 2019 small tar balls and oil slicks showed up affecting eleven states, mostly in the Northeast region, which depends mostly on tourism and fishery activities. The Brazilian National Contingency Plan (PNC) failed since the oil spread subsurface making impossible to see thorough satellite images. To help out to discover what happened, the Federal Government activated the Monitoring and Evaluation Group (GAA) to study different aspects of this disaster and learn how to prevent and mitigate a possible new event. This work is part of the Work Group 1, which deals with the Monitoring and Modeling, and describes the dataset composed of SAR images used to train the Artificial Intelligence (AI) and the future data that will be incorporated on the database of the project.

titulo: Deep learning automated workflow for cloud segmentation in remote sensing images
abstract: In this work, we propose an open source and automated workflow for semantic segmentation of remote sensing images. Even though it can be used in other sensors, to evaluate this workflow, a case study has been conducted applying a deep learning algorithm for segmenting clouds in images from WFI sensor, onboard CBERS-4A satellite. Since WFI does not have a tailor-made cloud segmentation algorithm, we customized our workflow based on the Unet neural network to fulfill this gap. Our results are promising according to our tests, although some problems were identified, like false positives over high albedo targets. These problems suggest improvements that could be tackled in the future.

titulo: A New Big Data Architecture for Efficient Processing of Spatiotemporal Data using Machine Learning
abstract: The volume of spatiotemporal data is increasing in the era of Big Data and has brought several challenges in managing and processing these data. Many big data platforms have been created to address these challenges, but none can outperform the others in all scenarios on spatiotemporal queries. This paper presents a scalable architecture for efficient spatiotemporal data processing. This architecture allows a couple of several big data platforms and automatically choose on the fly, using machine learning, the best big data platform to process each spatiotemporal query.

titulo: A tool to predict the growth of urban regions based on QGIS/MOLUSCE using MapBiomas image time series
abstract: This paper presents a method to generate future scenarios of Land- Use and Land-Cover (LULC) classification images by implementing an artificial neural network that can be used to predict urban growth. In this study, LULC data from 1985 to 2020 with annual intervals, obtained through MapBiomas, were used. These data were inserted into a neural network integrated with the MOLUSCE plugin from QGIS to model the possible spatio-temporal changes to simulate the evolution of LULC. MapBiomas is a powerful tool, that uses data from time series from Landsat Satellites and machine learning algorithms to provide reliable products. Our analysis focused on cities that have expanded greatly over the past two decades according to studies made by IBGE. The results obtained were better than those presented in related works, obtaining a kappa value of 0.74 and an accuracy value of at least 80% in all tests performed.

titulo: Spatiotemporal Representation of the Territorial Evolution of Brazil
abstract: This paper presents the modeling of a geographic database that includes temporal data on the evolution of territorial boundaries. Data on the changes that occurred in the Brazilian territory between the years of 1872 and 1991 are modified in order to implement the necessary structures for manipulation of temporal data. This works purpose is to achieve a single structure for analysis, comparison and visualization of spatiotemporal data. The result of the modeling is presented, along with an interaction in which it is possible to retrieve geographic data from a range of years. Keywords: temporal data, spatiotemporal data, geoprocessing, GIS, Brazilian territory, database modeling.

titulo: Generating POI type embeddings based on variableWord2Vec sentences
abstract: Point of Interest (POI) types are one of the most researched aspects on urban data. Several works have successfully modeled POI types cosidering POI co-occurrences in diferent spatial regions along with statistical models based on the Word2Vec technique from Natural Language Processing. Although these works have presented good results, they do not consider the spatial distance among related POIs as a feature to represent POI types. In this context, we present an approach based on Word2Vec sentences that includes such a distance to generate POI type embeddings, providing an improved POI type representation. Experiments based on similarity assessments between POI types revealed that our representation provides values close to human judgment.

titulo: Feature extraction of spatial panel data with autoencoders for clustering the Brazilian agricultural diversity
abstract: Brazilian agricultural production presents a high degree of spatial diversity, which challenges designing territorial public policies to promote sustainable development. This article proposes a new approach to cluster Brazilian municipalities according to their agricultural production. It combines a feature extraction mechanism using Deep Learning based on Autoencoders and clustering based on k-means and Self-Organizing Maps. We used the panel data from IBGEs annual estimates of Brazilian agricultural production between 1999 and 2018. Different structures of simple stacked undercomplete autoencoders were analyzed, varying the number of layers and neurons in each of them, including the latent layer. We evaluated the asymmetric exponential linear loss function to cope with the sparse data. The results show that in comparison with the ground truth adopted, the autoencoder model combined with the Self-Organizing Maps and the k-means algorithm presented a better result than the clustering of the raw data from the k-means, demonstrating the ability of simple stacked autoencoders to reduce the dimensionality and create a new space of features in their latent layer where the data can be analyzed and clustered. Although the general accuracy is low, the results are promising, considering that we can add new improvements to the Deep Clustering process.

titulo: Integrando mï¿½todos de anï¿½lise durante a coleta de amostras de uso e cobertura da terra
abstract: The Land Use and Land Cover (LULC) mapping based on machine learning requires collecting samples for the training of predictive models. This step is a time-consuming and expensive process, in addition this activity is subject to mislabeling samples. Therefore, several methods for quality assessment have been developed and applied successfully, but attaching the collect process with assessment becomes a challenge on some platforms. This article presents a work in progress that aims to integrate methods for sample analysis during the collection process in the TerraCollect system under development in the Brazil Data Cube. RESUMO: A produï¿½ï¿½o de mapas de uso e cobertura da terra com base em aprendizado de mï¿½quina requer a coleta de amostras para treinamento de modelos preditivos. Esta etapa ï¿½ custosa e pode demandar tempo, alï¿½m de ser uma atividade sujeita a erros na rotulaï¿½ï¿½o. Por conta disso, abordagens para anï¿½lise da qualidade de dados de treinamento tï¿½m sido desenvolvidas e empregadas com sucesso, porï¿½m unir estas duas atividades ainda ï¿½ um desafio em diversas plataformas. Este artigo apresenta um trabalho em andamento que busca a integraï¿½ï¿½o de mï¿½todos para a anï¿½lise das amostras durante a coleta na plataforma web TerraCollect em desenvolvimento no projeto Brazil Data Cube.

titulo: Modeling the dynamics of moving regions for knowledge discovery
abstract: Several methods to pattern mining in trajectories have been developed in the past years, most of them conceived for moving points. Nevertheless, there are potential demands to analyze the dynamic behavior of features with spatial extents, such as the changes in the land cover and weather imagery. This paper aims to extend both methods and patterns used in trajectory data mining defined for moving points to analyze moving regions. The proposed solution includes the transformation of the set of regions into a set of trajectories, the computation of the geometric differences, and the temporal analysis of both size and complexity of the shapes of such geometries.

titulo: Innovative Tools for TerraHidro: An Intuitive GIS User Interface
abstract: This article shows the development and implementation of the TerraHidro system, interface version, in Linux, with innovations in relation to the traditional interfaces of geographic information systems. The objective is to reduce user interaction with the system, allowing several functions to be performed with just one interaction, with intermediate functions being performed automatically. Therefore, it was necessary to create a metadata structure to associate each data with its type. In addition, a tool was implemented to save the execution history, under user command. A text file will be created. It will work with a TerraHidro function script in the command line version.

titulo: Mapeamento de Corte Seletivo na Amazï¿½nia Brasileira Usando Imagens Planet de Alta Resoluï¿½ï¿½o Espacial e Inteligï¿½ncia Artificial: estudo de caso na Floresta Nacional do Jamari
abstract: The Amazon has the largest remnant of tropical forests in the world and is being threatened daily by deforestation and forest degradation. The estimated area of forest degradation is underestimated, which is problematic for both sustainable policy enforcement, environmental oversight and national carbon emission inventories. Therefore, a deep learning model (U-Net) was trained to map forest degradation using Planet imagery (4.77 m spatial resolution) in the Jamari National Forest at the Brazilian Amazon. Preliminary results showed an overall accuracy of 67%. Our approach is promising to monitor forest concessions in Amazonia. Resumo. A Amazï¿½nia possui o maior remanescente de florestas tropicais do mundo e estï¿½ sendo ameaï¿½ada diariamente pelo desmatamento e degradaï¿½ï¿½o florestal. A ï¿½rea estimada de degradaï¿½ï¿½o florestal ï¿½ subestimada, o que ï¿½ problemï¿½tico tanto para a aplicaï¿½ï¿½o de polï¿½ticas sustentï¿½veis, fiscalizaï¿½ï¿½o ambiental e inventï¿½rios nacionais de emissï¿½es de carbono. Dessa forma, um modelo de deep learning (UNet) foi treinado para mapear a degradaï¿½ï¿½o florestal usando imagens Planet (resoluï¿½ï¿½o espacial de 4,77 m) na Floresta Nacional do Jamari, na Amazï¿½nia brasileira. Os resultados preliminares mostraram precisï¿½o geral de 67%. Nossa abordagem ï¿½ promissora para monitoramento de concessï¿½es florestais na Amazï¿½nia.

titulo: Uma metodologia para apoio ï¿½ tomada de decisï¿½o em cenï¿½rios de desastres ambientais utilizando dados pï¿½blicos
abstract: Environmental disasters, such as dam failures, cause impacts that go beyond the area of occurrence. From the region of origin to its arrival at sea, the sediments can cause environmental and economic impacts. Searching for ways to help in the recovery of these degraded areas, this work proposes the development of a methodology in that users utilize a Knowledge Discovery in Database Process (KDD) to group cities affected by environmental disasters, thus presenting the generation of knowledge groups with the same characteristics. In addition, reflecting all aspects of the proposed methodology, the development of an application that can be made available to the end user is planned. We hope that the proposed framework can be useful to support expert decision-making in this context. RESUMO: Desastres ambientais, como o rompimento de barragens, causam impactos que vï¿½o muito alï¿½m da ï¿½rea de ocorrï¿½ncia. Da regiï¿½o de origem atï¿½ a sua chegada ao mar, os resï¿½duos podem causar tanto impactos ambientais quanto econï¿½micos. Buscando formas de auxiliar na recuperaï¿½ï¿½o dessas ï¿½reas degradadas, neste trabalho, ï¿½ proposto o desenvolvimento de uma metodologia utilizando o processo de Descoberta de Conhecimento em Bases de Dados (Knowledge Discovery in Database  KDD) a fim de agrupar cidades afetadas por desastres ambientais, gerando assim grupos de cidades com caracterï¿½sticas semelhantes. Alï¿½m disso, refletindo toda a metodologia proposta, planeja-se o desenvolvimento de uma aplicaï¿½ï¿½oo que possa ser disponibilizada para o usuï¿½rio final. Esperamos que o arcabouï¿½o proposto possa ser ï¿½til para suportar a tomada de decisï¿½es de especialistas neste contexto.

titulo: Analysis of mobility network metrics in the spread of COVID-19 in the Metropolitan Region of Vale do Paraï¿½Ä±ba and Litoral Norte (RMVPLN)
abstract: In 2019, a pandemic of the so-called new coronavirus (SARS-COV-II) began, which causes the disease COVID-19. In a short time after the first case appeared, hundreds of countries began to register new cases every day. Mapping and analyzing the flow of people, regardless of the mode of transport, can help us to understand and prevent several phenomena that can affect our society in different ways. Graphs are complex networks made up of points and edges. The (geo)graphs are graphs with known spatial location and, in the case of our study, the edges represent the flow between them. The (geo)graphs proved to be a promising tool for such analyses. In the study region, municipalities that first registered their COVID-19 cases are also municipalities that have the highest mobility indices analyzed: degree, betweenness and weight of edges.

titulo: Vazios de Informaï¿½ï¿½o & Informalidade: Web-Scraping para Identificaï¿½ï¿½o de Assentamentos Precï¿½rios
abstract: This article presents an exploratory study to test the use of data from the construction of alternative web models for the identification of precarious settlements. The informal character of real estate transactions in AP, in which AP properties predominate, to the low density and absence of specialized property advertisements on the web, associated with the presence of AP. Thus, such information gaps represent information relevant to the identification of AP. The methodology used consists of mining 21,067 real estate advertisements in the city of Sï¿½o Bernardo do Campo (SP) on the Imï¿½velWeb portal. From the data, logistic models were built to estimate the probability of PA presence. The tests carried out confirmed that, as dynamic, the formal real estate markets were formed in a localized way in the territory and mostly in the perimeters of the AP, confirming an initial hypothesis Resumo. Este artigo apresenta um estudo exploratï¿½rio para testar a utilizaï¿½ï¿½o de dados alternativos, provenientes de web-scraping na construï¿½ï¿½o de modelos voltados para a identificaï¿½ï¿½o de assentamentos precï¿½rios (AP). Dado o carï¿½ter informal das transaï¿½ï¿½es imobiliï¿½rias em AP, nos quais predominam situaï¿½ï¿½es de inseguranï¿½a da posse, partiu-se da hipï¿½tese de que ï¿½reas caracterizadas por baixa densidade e atï¿½ mesmo ausï¿½ncia de anï¿½ncios de imï¿½veis em portais web especializados, estï¿½o associadas ï¿½ presenï¿½a de AP. Assim, tais vazios de informaï¿½ï¿½o representam, na verdade, informaï¿½ï¿½o relevante para a identificaï¿½ï¿½o de AP. A metodologia utilizada consistiu na mineraï¿½ï¿½o de 21.067 anï¿½ncios imobiliï¿½rios da cidade de Sï¿½o Bernardo do Campo (SP) no portal Imï¿½velWeb. A partir desses dados, foram construï¿½dos modelos logï¿½sticos para estimar a probabilidade de presenï¿½a de AP. Os testes realizados confirmaram que as dinï¿½micas do mercado imobiliï¿½rios formal se dï¿½o de forma concentrada no territï¿½rio e majoritariamente fora dos perï¿½metros dos AP, confirmando a hipï¿½tese inicial do trabalho.

titulo: Analysis of the watersheds of National Interconnected System (SIN) using Complex Network
abstract: Os recursos hï¿½dricos no Brasil sï¿½o uma das maiores fontes de geraï¿½ï¿½o de energia do paï¿½s. O Sistema Interligado Nacional (SIN) ï¿½ constituï¿½do por diversas bacias que sï¿½o interligadas. Acompanhar e monitorar estes reservatï¿½rios de ï¿½gua ï¿½ de grande importï¿½ncia para o Brasil. Ao longo dos anos ï¿½ possï¿½vel identificar alteraï¿½ï¿½es no sistema hï¿½drico devido a fatores climï¿½ticos, como as mudanï¿½as climaticas. Este trabalho tem como objetivo identificar a conexï¿½o entre as bacias hidrogrï¿½ficas que constituem o SIN, a correlaï¿½ï¿½o entre as suas series histï¿½ricas de Energia Natural Afluente (ENA) utilizando tï¿½cnicas de redes complexas. Por meio deste trabalho ï¿½ possï¿½vel identificar as bacias que possuem maior nï¿½mero conexï¿½es e que sï¿½o de elevada importï¿½ncia no sistema, as suas caracterï¿½sticas influenciam nos fatores de outras bacias. Resumo. Water resources in Brazil are one of the largest sources of energy generation in the country. The National Interconnected System (SIN) consists of several watersheds that are interconnected. Tracking and monitoring these water reservoirs is of great importance for Brazil. Over the years it is possible to identify changes in the water system due to climatic factors, such as climate change. This work aims to identify the connection between the hydrographic watersheds that constitute the SIN, the correlation between their historical series of Affluent Natural Energy (ENA) using complex network techniques. Through this work it is possible to identify the watersheds that higher degree connections and that are of high importance in the system, their characteristics influence the factors of other watersheds.

titulo: Agrupamento de endereï¿½os georreferenciados para a delimitaï¿½ï¿½o de ï¿½reas postais
abstract: The Postal Addressing Code (CEP) is a form of sectorized representation of residential and commercial units within the Brazilian territory and is most used by logistics companies and mail services. This article aims to provide a spatial database that includes a representation of CEP areas belonging to the State of Minas Gerais based on a data clustering of data from residential and commercial addresses. From this spatial representation, the regions covered by each CEP can be materialized and used as a strategy for approximation in geocoding process. RESUMO: O Cï¿½digo de Endereï¿½amento Postal (CEP) ï¿½ uma forma de representaï¿½ï¿½o setorizada de unidades residenciais e comerciais dentro do territï¿½rio brasileiro, sendo amplamente utilizado por empresas de logï¿½stica e serviï¿½os postais. Este artigo tem como objetivo fornecer uma base de dados espaciais que inclua uma representaï¿½ï¿½o geomï¿½trica para ï¿½reas de CEP pertencentes ao Estado de Minas Gerais a partir de um agrupamento de dados dos endereï¿½os das residï¿½ncias e comï¿½rcios. A partir dessa representaï¿½ï¿½o espacial, as regiï¿½es correspondentes ï¿½ ï¿½rea de cada CEP serï¿½o estimadas e exploradas como estratï¿½gia de aproximaï¿½ï¿½o no processo de geocodificaï¿½ï¿½o.

titulo: Controle da Qualidade Cartogrï¿½fica de Basemaps: Avaliaï¿½ï¿½o da Completude
abstract: This work aimed to evaluate the completeness of the OpenStreetMap, Google Road, Bing Map and CARTO Voyager basemaps in the central area of the Federal University of Viï¿½osa (Viï¿½osa-MG), considering nine layers, and the omission and commission subelements presented by ET-CQDG. With the results obtained, a non-conforming result was found in relation to the omission in all layers in all basemaps, which did not occur in relation to the commission, since in some layers some basemaps presented a conforming result. Thus, it was noted the need to improve the information mainly absent from these basemaps in these areas, aiming at a better representation of the real world. Resumo. Este trabalho teve como objetivo avaliar a completude dos basemaps OpenStreetMap, Google Road, Bing Map e CARTO Voyager na ï¿½rea central da Universidade Federal de Viï¿½osa (Viï¿½osa-MG), considerando nove camadas, e os subelementos omissï¿½o e comissï¿½o apresentados pela ET-CQDG. Com os resultados obtidos, constatou-se um resultado nï¿½o conforme em relaï¿½ï¿½o ï¿½ omissï¿½o em todas as camadas em todos os basemaps, o que nï¿½o ocorreu em relaï¿½ï¿½o ï¿½ comissï¿½o, visto que em algumas camadas alguns basemaps apresentaram um resultado conforme. Dessa forma, notou-se a necessidade de melhoria das informaï¿½ï¿½es principalmente ausentes desses basemaps nessa ï¿½rea, visando uma melhor representaï¿½ï¿½o do mundo real.

titulo: Daily Weather Pattern for Sï¿½ao Paulo city: from 1st January to 31st March, 2019
abstract: This paper presents the list of weather pattern categories over Sao Paulo city for every day from 1st January to 31st March. The convective weather pattern is the most frequent (42%), followed by the absence of weather pattern (36%). Although cold fronts, troughs, South Atlantic Convergence Zone and Moisture Convergence Zone weather patterns correspond to a fewer number of days, their impacts in urban mobility can be significant. Therefore, the results of this paper can be useful in future studies correlating, for example, this list of weather pattern categories with urban flash floods. That could contribute to improve flash flood warnings and mitigate their impacts.

titulo: Modeling and Validation of Elevation Data Obtained by GNSS and Drone Devices for Managing Floodings in Coastal Lands Caused by Climate Change
abstract: This short-paper reports and explores a methodology to model and validate geographical elevation information from samples, points and images, acquired by Global Network Satellite System (GNSS) and Aerial Drone devices. The elevation models will be used on flooding management activities of coastal lands caused by climate change. The methodology of this article addresses details: on how to obtain and process GNSS sample points and drone images to generate the final Digital Elevation Model (DEM) and; on how to validate the DEM information to attain its quantitative quality. Also, the article presents some initial ideas for creating different flooding scenarios according to the sea level variation. The simulated scenarios can be used on decision making activities to minimize risks of human and material losses. The up-to-now results, obtained in a case study in Guaecï¿½a beach area, show the applicability and the feasibility of the proposed methodology.

titulo: The effects of COVID-19 on Chinese commuting patterns in early 2020
abstract: We build mobility networks from Chinese commuting data and track network metrics for the two months before the WHO pandemic announcement. TheWuhan travel ban on 23 January imposed changes to the level of importance of some central cities in the commuting patterns. While Beijing was the most important city in both the inflows and outflows, Wuhan and other cities became more relevant after the transition.

titulo: Rain-related Tweets in flooding days: a brief analysis on Tamanduateï¿½ river basin
abstract: Flooding has become an increasingly frequent phenomenon and reason for several economic and human losses worldwide, such as the Sao Paulo city, Brazil. The literature has shown that Voluntary Geographic Information (VGI) data is a valuable complementary source for monitoring and analyzing hydrological and meteorological events. Face with this motivation, we extracted VGI from the Twitter social network and analyzed the frequency of meteorological-related posts before flooding/non-flooding events and its correlation with physical-meteorological measures. The obtained results indicate that Twitter is a valuable VGI source for monitoring flooding events.

titulo: A convolutional LSTM neural network for precipitation nowcasting based on weather radar data
abstract: Due to the high instability of the atmosphere, it is a challenging task to forecast precipitation events in the very short term. Artificial Neural Networks have been recently employed in this context as an alternative to solve prediction problems. This work proposes a convolutional LSTM neural network based on weather radar images (spatial data) to forecast rainfall events, whatever the precipitation intensity is. The preliminary results show better performance when comparing the network prediction error with a persistence model.

titulo: Padrï¿½es espaï¿½o-temporais da ocorrï¿½ncia do fogo na Amazï¿½nia brasileira
abstract: The occurrence of fires in the Amazon can be amplified by severe droughts and the accumulation of combustible material due to deforestation and forest degradation. This study aimed to identify the pattern of occurrence of hotspots between 2003 and 2019, with emphasis on the fire day. Hotspot data were used to identify spatio-temporal patterns of hotspot occurrence and positive fire anomalies in the Brazilian Amazon biome. The results showed peaks in the occurrence of hotspots in 2005 and 2007, with respectively 85,721 and 90,206 hotspots observed during the month of September. About 55.3% of the biome area has a monthly recurrence of positive fire anomalies for up to 3 months. Resumo. A ocorrï¿½ncia de incï¿½ndios na Amazï¿½nia pode ser amplificada por secas severas e o acï¿½mulo de material combustï¿½vel devido ao desmatamento e ï¿½ degradaï¿½ï¿½o florestal. Este estudo teve como objetivo identificar o padrï¿½o de ocorrï¿½ncia de focos de calor entre 2003 e 2019, com destaque para o dia do fogo, nesse ï¿½ltimo ano. Foram utilizados dados de focos de calor para identificar padrï¿½es espaï¿½o-temporais da ocorrï¿½ncia do fogo e as anomalias positivas desses eventos no bioma amazï¿½nico brasileiro. Os resultados demonstraram picos na ocorrï¿½ncia de focos de calor em 2005 e 2007, com respectivamente 85.721 e 90.206 focos observados durante o mï¿½s de setembro. Cerca de 55,3% da ï¿½rea do bioma apresenta recorrï¿½ncia mensal de anomalias positivas de fogo de atï¿½ 3 meses.

titulo: Contribuiï¿½ï¿½o de produtos de ï¿½rea queimada na Amazï¿½nia Maranhense: proposta de avaliaï¿½ï¿½es combinadas
abstract: The fusion of fire products can significantly refine the identification of burned areas, and based on that, we evaluated the possibility of using three products, MCD64A1, GABAM and Fire_CCI, for the years 2012 to 2019 in the Maranhï¿½o Amazon region. We succeeded in increasing the identification of the burned area of 24%, reaching up to 50% more than the MCD64A1, a product that would have the best coverage result for the region. Resumo. A fusï¿½o de produtos de fogo podem refinar significativamente a identificaï¿½ï¿½o de ï¿½reas queimadas, e partindo disso avaliamos a possibilidade de uso de trï¿½s produtos, MCD64A1, GABAM e Fire_CCI, para os anos de 2012 a 2019 na amazï¿½nia maranhense. Conseguimos trazer aumentos na identificaï¿½ï¿½o da ï¿½rea queimada de 24% podendo chegar atï¿½ 50% a mais que o MCD64A1, produto que teria o melhor resultado de cobertura para a regiï¿½o.

titulo: Acessibilidade a Escolas na Regiï¿½o do Grande ABC: Desigualdades Socioespaciais
abstract: This study explores the spatial inequalities of access to schools through walking. Two accessibility metrics  Cumulative and Balanced Floating Catchment Area (BFCA)  were applied in the Greater ABC Region, Sï¿½o Paulo, to understand their potential and limitations. It was observed that although the cumulative metric does not consider the competition between opportunities, while the BFCA considers it, the spatial results of the two metrics were complementary in the studied region. Furthermore, the two metrics showed that the correlation between accessibility and income is negative for lower-income groups and positive for higher-income groups. Thus, it was found that spatial analysis from the use of such measures can be an important tool to support the formulation of public policies territorially oriented. Resumo. Este estudo explora as desigualdades espaciais de acesso ï¿½s escolas atravï¿½s da caminhada. Duas mï¿½tricas de acessibilidade  Cumulativa e Balanced Floating Catchment Area (BFCA) - foram aplicadas na Regiï¿½o do Grande ABC, Sï¿½o Paulo, a fim de compreender suas potencialidades e limitaï¿½ï¿½es. Observou-se que apesar de a mï¿½trica cumulativa nï¿½o levar em conta a competiï¿½ï¿½o entre oportunidades, ao passo que a BFCA a considera, os resultados espaciais dos dois ï¿½ndices foram complementares na regiï¿½o estudada. Ademais, as duas mï¿½tricas apontaram que a correlaï¿½ï¿½o entre acessibilidade e renda ï¿½ negativa para grupos de menor renda e positiva para grupos de maior renda. Assim, constatou-se que a anï¿½lise espacial a partir do uso de tais medidas pode ser uma importante ferramenta para subsidiar a formulaï¿½ï¿½o de polï¿½ticas pï¿½blicas territorialmente orientadas.

titulo: Deforestation Increases Within Full Protected Areas of the Brazilian Amazon
abstract: Since 2012, deforestation rates have been increasing within the Brazilian Amazon. This situation undermines the mitigation efforts against global climate change and stresses our need for both accurate identification of deforestation hotspots and promotion of law enforcement. It also implies a permanent need to explore the deforestation data, looking for patterns that enable the public, the academia, and authorities to anticipate and prepare for deforestation outburst. For this reason, we analyzed data of the Brazilian deforestation monitoring program (PRODES) to find deforestation patterns within Full Protected Areas of the Brazilian Amazon. Our aim is to identify and quantify deforestation from 2008 to 2021. We applied a trend analysis to find FPAs with significant increments and we found that approximately 15% of FPAs have statistically significant deforestation growth according to the Mann-Kendall test at 5% significance. Furthermore, we found that only four FPAs accounted for 67.6% of the deforestation increments during 2021, making them good candidates for closer monitoring and law enforcement. Besides, our results also show the role of FPAs as forest safeguards, although deforestation within them is increasing.

titulo: Towards generating a traffic slowness geospatial dataset of Sï¿½ao Paulo city
abstract: The city of Sao Paulo is known for its extensive fleet of vehicles, which, combined with extreme precipitation and flooding events, makes traffic increasingly chaotic. The present work comprises an extracting, transforming and loading (ETL) process to generate a database of 2019 traffic slowness in Sao Paulo integrated with variables that may influence traffic, such as rainfall, floods, accidents and socioeconomic features. This dataset is expected to support more profound analysis and help in better traffic planning and decision-making.

titulo: Implementaï¿½ï¿½o de Ferramentas em Software Livre para Avaliaï¿½ï¿½o da Acurï¿½cia Posicional em Produtos Cartogrï¿½ficos
abstract: In Brazil, there are standards for evaluating one of the most important elements of cartographic quality control, which is positional accuracy. Among them, the following stand out: Decree No. 89,817 of 1984 and the Technical Specification for Quality Control of Geospatial Data (ET-CQDG) of the National Spatial Data Infrastructure (INDE); the INCRA Normative Instruction of 2018 and NBR 13,133 of 1994. However, there is a scarcity of cartographic quality control tools implemented in free-form Geographic Information System (GIS) software. Thus, this work presents the development of a plugin in QGIS for the assessment of positional accuracy of cartographic data and products based on current regulations in the country. Resumo. No Brasil existem normas visando a avaliaï¿½ï¿½o de um dos elementos mais importantes do controle de qualidade cartogrï¿½fica, que ï¿½ a acurï¿½cia posicional. Dentre elas, destacam-se: Decreto nï¿½ 89.817 de 1984 e a Especificaï¿½ï¿½o Tï¿½cnica de Controle de Qualidade de Dados Geoespaciais (ETCQDG) da Infraestrutura Nacional de Dados Espaciais (INDE); a Instruï¿½ï¿½o Normativa do INCRA de 2018 e a NBR 13.133 de 1994. Entretanto, hï¿½ uma escassez de ferramentas de controle de qualidade cartogrï¿½fica implementadas em softwares de Sistema de Informaï¿½ï¿½o Geogrï¿½fica (SIG) de forma livre. Assim, este trabalho apresenta o desenvolvimento de um plugin no QGIS para a avaliaï¿½ï¿½o da acurï¿½cia posicional de dados e produtos cartogrï¿½ficos com base nas normas vigentes no paï¿½s.

titulo: Development of a data set for distributed hydrological simulation of Paraiba do Sul Basin
abstract: This work describes a dataset created for the purpose of simulating the Paraiba do Sul basin with hydrological distributed models. The dataset contains gridded numerical and descriptive information related to different characteristics of the basin. Distributed hydrological models are useful tools for the management of water resources, as they allow estimating the hydrological regime in a basin according to its physical, rainfall and land use change characteristics, seeking to accurately quantify the availability of water allowing planning future use according to scenarios of land use change or climate change.

titulo: Sistema de Geracï¿½ ï¿½ao de Dados Faltantes em Sï¿½eries Temporais Climatolï¿½ogicas usando Triangulacï¿½ ï¿½ao, Aprendizado e Meta-Aprendizado de Mï¿½aquinas
abstract: This work presents an application developed for the generation of missing data in time series, using meteorological data as input parameters. The application uses data from a set of four different cities, containing three climate indicators: maximum temperature, minimum temperature and precipitation. The proposed tool is able not only to complete time series that have data gaps, but also to generate a complete time series for a city that does not have any data. The system uses data triangulation, machine learning and meta-learning algorithms. RESUMO: Este trabalho apresenta uma aplicaï¿½ï¿½o desenvolvida para a geraï¿½ï¿½o de dados ausentes em sï¿½ries temporais, usando como parï¿½metros de entrada dados meteorolï¿½gicos. A aplicaï¿½ï¿½o utiliza dados de um conjunto de quatro cidades distintas, contendo trï¿½s indicadores climï¿½ticos: temperatura mï¿½xima, temperatura mï¿½nima e precipitaï¿½ï¿½o. A ferramenta proposta ï¿½ capaz nï¿½o sï¿½ de completar sï¿½ries temporais que possuam lacunas de dados, como tambï¿½m gerar uma sï¿½rie temporal completa para uma cidade que nï¿½o possua nenhum dado. O sistema utiliza algoritmos de triangulaï¿½ï¿½o de dados, aprendizado e metaaprendizado de mï¿½quinas.

titulo: Um Mï¿½todo de Verificaï¿½ï¿½o de Conformidade do Plantio Realizado no Contexto do Seguro Rural no Brasil com Zoneamento Agrï¿½cola de Risco Climï¿½tico (ZARC)
abstract: Abstract. The Agricultural Zoning of Climate Risk (ZARC) is an agricultural policy, which identifies regions and moments of lower climate risk for planting crops. The Rural Insurance Subsidy program (PSR) is an important instrument of Brazilian agricultural policy, as it grants insurance subsidies to rural properties. To be entitled to the federal subsidy for the rural insurance premium, the rural producer must respect the sowing or planting data recommended by the ZARC for the municipality. The present work of agricultural cultivation affirms information about the data of rural cultivation and the ZARC, a safer mode of production for the production of crops and guarantees agricultural production, with less. Resumo. O Zoneamento Agrï¿½cola de Risco Climï¿½tico (ZARC) ï¿½ uma plataforma de polï¿½tica agrï¿½cola que define regiï¿½es e ï¿½pocas de menor risco climï¿½tico para plantio das culturas. O programa de Subvenï¿½ï¿½o ao Seguro Rural (PSR) ï¿½ um instrumento que concede subvenï¿½ï¿½o ao Prï¿½mio de Seguro Rural para propriedades rurais. Para fazer jus ï¿½ subvenï¿½ï¿½o, o produtor deve respeitar as datas de semeadura ou plantio preconizadas pelo ZARC. O presente trabalho propï¿½e um algoritmo para cruzamento entre as bases de dados do seguro rural e da plataforma ZARC, de modo a fornecer informaï¿½ï¿½es sobre os perï¿½odos mais favorï¿½veis para plantio das culturas agrï¿½colas e garantir uma produï¿½ï¿½o agrï¿½cola mais assertiva, com menos perdas.

titulo: Uso do Google Earth Engine como instrumento de identificaï¿½ï¿½o de anomalias climï¿½ticas no Brasil
abstract: Due to the increase in extreme weather and climate events, this paper aims to identify the anomalies caused by such climatic variability and how they influence the agricultural sector, especially in the region known as MATOPIBA (Maranhï¿½o, Tocantins, Piauï¿½ e Bahia). For the climate analysis, reanalysis data from the European Center for Medium-Range Weather Forecasts (ECMWF) called Era5, with a spatial resolution of 11,132 meters, available in the Google Earth Engine database, were used. The results demonstrate that the water storage capacity in the soil, especially in the initial layers, is a variable of paramount importance for agricultural crops, as it includes climatic and edaphic aspects of the environment. Resumo. Diante do aumento de eventos extremos de tempo e clima, o presente artigo tem como objetivo identificar as anomalias causadas por tais variabilidades climï¿½ticas e como as mesmas influenciam no setor agrï¿½rio, principalmente na regiï¿½o conhecida como MATOPIBA (Maranhï¿½o, Tocantins, Piauï¿½ e Bahia). Para a anï¿½lise climï¿½tica utilizaram-se os dados de reanï¿½lise do European Centre for Medium-Range Weather Forecasts (ECMWF) denominado Era5, com resoluï¿½ï¿½o espacial de 11.132 metros, disponï¿½vel na base de dados do Google Earth Engine. Os resultados encontrados demonstram que a capacidade de armazenamento de ï¿½gua no solo, principalmente nas camadas iniciais, ï¿½ uma variï¿½vel de suma importï¿½ncia para as culturas agrï¿½colas, pois contempla aspectos climï¿½ticos e edï¿½ficos do ambiente.

titulo: Statistical Analysis of a Spatially Explicit Hydrological Forecasting Model using Remote Sensing of the Atmosphere
abstract: The performance analysis of a data based machine learning model depends directly on the separation of the data for training and testing, thus, a single performance evaluation can generate a biased results. Taking this into account, the aim of this paper is to propose and exemplify a statistical analysis method to get a proper understanding of a spatially explicit hydrological forecasting model performance. For this, the weather radar data used to training and testing a neural network based model was splitted randomically 100 times, and the RMSE and NASH metric was calculated for each one. The analysis of the metrics frequency distribution indicates that the neural network model had better performance than the persistence one.

titulo: Mapeamento da susceptibilidade ï¿½ incï¿½ndios atravï¿½s de Sensoriamento Remoto no municï¿½pio de Cï¿½ceres
abstract: Recently, fire events recorded in Brazil have motivated the application of Remote Sensing and Machine Learning techniques in order to enable the monitoring and prevention of the impacts associated with these events. In this way, the present project aims to map the susceptibility to fires in forest regions through the application of Logistic Regression, built on the basis of the extraction of statistical descriptors under spectral indices. RESUMO: Recentemente os eventos de queimada registrados no Brasil tem motivado a aplicaï¿½ï¿½o de tï¿½cnicas de Sensoriamento Remoto e Aprendizado de Mï¿½quina a fim de possibilitar o monitoramento e a prevenï¿½ï¿½o dos impactos associados ï¿½ estes eventos. Tendo isso em vista, o presente projeto tem como objetivo mapear a susceptibilidade ï¿½ incï¿½ndios em regiï¿½es de floresta atravï¿½s da aplicaï¿½ï¿½o de Regressï¿½o Logï¿½stica, construï¿½dos com base na extraï¿½ï¿½o de descritores estatï¿½sticos sob ï¿½ndices espectrais.

titulo: Mobility Database of the Metropolitan Region of Belo Horizonte, Brazil
abstract: This paper describes the mobility database of the metropolitan region of Belo Horizonte, Brazil. It comprises the official data from the origin and destination survey carried out in 2012, 2019 and 2021 and it is available on GitHub online repository. It contains vector files (shapefiles) and alphanumeric files (.txt and .xlsx). Shapefiles refers to: municipalities, macro mobility unit, homogeneous areas, traffic zones and census sectors boundaries and the road system. The geography coordinate system is EPSG 4326  SIRGAS2000.

titulo: An urban flood database for Disaster Risk Reduction studies in Sï¿½o Paulo, Brazil
abstract: This paper describes the urban flood database of an area in the Sï¿½o Paulo municipality, Brazil. The database is organized into input, processed, output and auxiliary data, and it contains vector files (shapefiles) and alphanumeric files. The input data have their original spatial topology. The processed data presents the spatialization of alphanumeric data as well as generated by-products. The output data presents the variables aggregated in a regular grid. It is available on GitHub online repository. The geography coordinate system is EPSG 4326  SIRGAS2000.

titulo: Mapeamento colaborativo do nï¿½vel de ruï¿½do em Guayaquil durante o COVID-19
abstract: The SARS-CoV-2 virus triggered a never-before-seen scenario worldwide. Ecuador declared a state of emergency to contain coronavirus transmission by applying social isolation policies and mobility limitations. During this period, noise pollution decreased in Guayaquil city. This study proposes the use of smartphones to collect noise data and generate soundscape maps of Guayaquil during different time slots. The results revealed traffic as the predominant type of noise, followed by vehicles, buses, and peopleï¿½s voices. This article demonstrates the feasibility of crowdsourcing for the collection of environmental variables. RESUMO: O vï¿½rus SARS-CoV-2 desencadeou um cenï¿½rio nunca antes visto no mundo. No Equador, um Estado de Exceï¿½ï¿½o foi declarado para tentar conter a transmissï¿½o do coronavï¿½rus, aplicando polï¿½ticas de isolamento social e limitaï¿½ï¿½es de mobilidade. Nesse contexto, evidenciou-se uma diminuiï¿½ï¿½o da poluiï¿½ï¿½o sonora na cidade de Guayaquil, Guayas. Neste estudo, propï¿½e-se o uso de smartphones para a coleta colaborativa de dados de ruï¿½dos e geraï¿½ï¿½o de mapas da paisagem sonora em Guayaquil, para diferentes faixas horï¿½rias. Os resultados revelaram que o tipo de ruï¿½do predominante ï¿½ o trï¿½fego de automï¿½veis, seguidos pelas vozes das pessoas. Este artigo demonstra a viabilidade do crowdsourcing para a coleta de variï¿½veis ambientais.

titulo: WebODM para o Processamento de Imagens Aï¿½reas Obtidas por Drone em uma ï¿½rea Rural: como alternativa aos softwares proprietï¿½rios
abstract: Drones and their various applications have been gaining great space around the world. In remote sensing, drones excel in collecting information with high resolution and accuracy. To generate these information, it is necessary to process the images with specific photogrammetry software. However, most of these software are proprietary with high prices in restrictive licenses, thus making it difficult for some groups to access this technology. This work aims to evaluate the free software WebODM to process images obtained by drone in a rural area, as an alternative to proprietary software. Our results have shown that WebODM can be used as a feasible alternative to obtain orthomosaics and DEM as high-quality products to photointerpretation. Resumo. Os drones e suas diversas aplicaï¿½ï¿½es vï¿½m ganhando grande espaï¿½o ao redor do mundo. No sensoriamento remoto, os drones se destacam na coleta de informaï¿½ï¿½es com alta resoluï¿½ï¿½o e precisï¿½o. Para gerar essas informaï¿½ï¿½es, ï¿½ necessï¿½rio processar as imagens com software especï¿½fico de fotogrametria. No entanto, a maioria desses softwares sï¿½o proprietï¿½rios com preï¿½os elevados em licenï¿½as restritivas, dificultando o acesso de alguns grupos a essa tecnologia. Este trabalho tem como objetivo avaliar o software livre WebODM para processamento de imagens obtidas por drone em uma ï¿½rea rural, como alternativa aos softwares proprietï¿½rios. Nossos resultados mostraram que o WebODM pode ser utilizado como uma alternativa viï¿½vel para obter ortomosaicos e DEM como produtos de alta qualidade para fotointerpretaï¿½ï¿½o.

titulo: OpenStreetMap Tools for Humanitarian Aid
abstract: This paper describes a set of services based on OSM developed for humanitarian action at HeiGIT and GIScience Heidelberg. These are available at https://github.com/GIScience. In this tools paper we describe the latest developments for selected tools, like the OpenStreetMap history analysis framework (ohsome.org) and the mobility platform openrouteservice.org and their respective usage in new services like the ohsome quality analyst OQT, the global access healthcare map or a new HeiGIT disaster portal API.
